title: Add Terraform scaffold and state backend
description: Create the Terraform infra/ folder structure, module skeletons, dev and prod environments, and a bootstrap script to create the S3 state bucket and DynamoDB lock table. Does not touch template.yaml or any existing application files.

acceptance_criteria.given: the infra/ folder does not exist
acceptance_criteria.then: infra/modules/ contains dynamodb/, lambda/, api-gateway/, s3-cloudfront/, ssm/, vpc/ directories each with main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf
dev/ and prod/ both exist each containing main.tf, variables.tf, terraform.tfvars, backend.tf
it prints the aws commands it would run without executing them
it succeeds with no errors
.terraform/, *.tfstate, *.tfstate.backup are all listed
0 HIGH or CRITICAL findings

non_functional_requirements.security: No secrets or account IDs hardcoded in any .tf file. State bucket must have versioning and encryption enabled.
non_functional_requirements.compliance: All resources must follow naming convention: {project}-{environment}-{resource}

definition_of_done: infra/ folder structure created with all modules and environments
terraform init succeeds in dev environment
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. If the constitution file does not exist, stop and write an escalation — do not proceed without it. Do not modify template.yaml or any files outside infra/, scripts/, .github/, and .gitignore.

title: Terraform DynamoDB module
description: Terraform module that creates the yooti-metrics DynamoDB table matching the SAM definition in template.yaml exactly. Single-table design, PAY_PER_REQUEST billing, TTL enabled, GSI-1 on project_id + updated_at.

acceptance_criteria.then: it has PK (HASH) and SK (RANGE) as the key schema matching template.yaml exactly
GSI-1 exists on project_id (HASH) and updated_at (RANGE) with ALL projection
TTL is enabled on the ttl attribute
Project, Environment, and ManagedBy=terraform tags are present
0 HIGH or CRITICAL findings
validation succeeds with no errors

non_functional_requirements.security: Point-in-time recovery enabled. Server-side encryption enabled. No public access.
non_functional_requirements.compliance: Table name must follow naming convention: {project}-{environment}-metrics

definition_of_done: Module creates table with correct key schema matching template.yaml
agent_instructions: Read template.yaml before writing the module — the DynamoDB resource defined there is the source of truth. Mirror it exactly in Terraform. Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files.

title: Terraform Lambda and API Gateway module
description: Terraform modules for all 6 Lambda functions and the API Gateway REST API, mirroring template.yaml. IAM roles with least-privilege DynamoDB permissions. Environment variables wired from SSM Parameter Store.

acceptance_criteria.then: all 6 functions exist: get_current_metrics, ingest_metrics, get_sprint_list, get_sprint_detail, get_trends, get_story_detail
each role grants only the DynamoDB actions it needs — no Action=[*] or Resource=[*]
all 6 routes match the SAM template exactly: GET /metrics/current, POST /metrics/ingest, GET /metrics/sprints, GET /metrics/sprint/{num}, GET /metrics/trends, GET /metrics/story/{id}
DYNAMODB_TABLE and AWS_REGION are set from SSM Parameter Store references

acceptance_criteria.when: running checkov on infra/modules/lambda/ and infra/modules/api-gateway/
acceptance_criteria.then: 0 HIGH or CRITICAL findings
validation succeeds with no errors

non_functional_requirements.security: Lambda execution roles use least privilege. No inline policies with wildcard actions. API Gateway has throttling configured.
non_functional_requirements.performance: Lambda memory set to 256MB for all functions. Timeout set to 30 seconds.

definition_of_done: All 6 Lambda functions defined in module
API Gateway routes match template.yaml exactly
agent_instructions: Read template.yaml before writing the module — it is the source of truth for Lambda handlers, routes, and IAM permissions. Mirror it exactly. Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. Do not use Action=[*] or Resource=[*] in any IAM policy.

title: Terraform S3 and CloudFront module for React frontend
description: Terraform module for React frontend hosting. Private S3 bucket with versioning and CloudFront distribution using Origin Access Control (OAC). HTTPS enforced, custom error page for SPA routing.

acceptance_criteria.then: public access is blocked on all 4 settings and the bucket is not publicly accessible
it uses Origin Access Control (OAC) not Origin Access Identity (OAI)

acceptance_criteria.when: accessing the CloudFront URL over HTTP
acceptance_criteria.then: it redirects to HTTPS with a 301 response

acceptance_criteria.when: accessing a non-existent path on the CloudFront URL
acceptance_criteria.then: it returns index.html with a 200 status code for SPA routing
versioning is enabled
0 HIGH or CRITICAL findings

non_functional_requirements.security: S3 bucket must block all public access. CloudFront must use OAC. TLS 1.2 minimum. WAF optional for prod.
non_functional_requirements.performance: CloudFront cache TTL: 1 year for hashed assets, no-cache for index.html.

definition_of_done: S3 bucket private with versioning enabled
Custom error page routes all 404s to index.html
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. Use OAC not OAI — OAI is deprecated. The S3 bucket must have zero public access. The CloudFront error page must return 200 not 404 for /index.html to support React Router.

title: Terraform SSM Parameter Store module
description: Terraform module that creates SSM Parameter Store entries for all environment variables the Lambda functions need. Values are placeholders — real values are set manually after apply. Secrets use SecureString type.

acceptance_criteria.then: /{project}/{env}/DYNAMODB_TABLE exists as a String parameter
/{project}/{env}/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY exists as a SecureString parameter
/{project}/{env}/CORS_ORIGINS and /{project}/{env}/AWS_REGION exist as String parameters
all values are placeholders (CHANGE_ME or similar) — no real secrets are committed
it documents the command to set real values for each parameter
0 HIGH or CRITICAL findings

non_functional_requirements.security: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and any other secrets must be SecureString. Never commit real values. Placeholder values must be obviously fake.

definition_of_done: All required parameters created with correct types
Placeholder values only — no real secrets
README documents how to set real values
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. Never use real secret values — always use CHANGE_ME or similar placeholder. SecureString type must be used for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and any other credentials. Write a README section documenting the aws ssm put-parameter commands to set real values.

title: Terraform dev environment wiring
description: Wire all Terraform modules together in infra/environments/dev/ with dev-appropriate settings. Remote state configured. terraform apply succeeds and creates all resources in AWS.

acceptance_criteria.then: it shows a clean plan with no errors and resources match expected count

acceptance_criteria.when: curling the API Gateway URL at /metrics/current
acceptance_criteria.then: it returns a 200 or 404 (not 5xx) proving the endpoint is reachable
it returns the React dashboard index.html
all follow the pattern yooti-dashboard-dev-{resource}
it shows 0 changes — state matches reality
0 HIGH or CRITICAL findings

non_functional_requirements.security: Dev environment uses same security controls as prod — no relaxed security for dev.
non_functional_requirements.cost: Lambda memory 128MB in dev. DynamoDB PAY_PER_REQUEST. No NAT Gateway unless required.

agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. This story wires all modules together — do not create new resources here, only call modules. Run the bootstrap script first to create the state backend before attempting terraform init.

title: Frontend deploy script and GitHub Actions workflow
description: npm run build + aws s3 sync deploy script and GitHub Actions workflow that deploys the React frontend to S3 and invalidates the CloudFront cache on every merge to main.

acceptance_criteria.then: the built frontend files are synced to the S3 bucket and a CloudFront invalidation is created

acceptance_criteria.given: a PR is merged to main
acceptance_criteria.then: it builds the frontend, syncs to S3, and invalidates CloudFront automatically
it sets correct cache headers: max-age=31536000 for hashed assets, no-cache for index.html
AWS credentials come from GitHub secrets, not hardcoded values
the latest version of the dashboard is served

non_functional_requirements.security: AWS credentials must come from GitHub OIDC or secrets — never hardcoded. S3 sync uses --delete flag to remove stale files.
non_functional_requirements.performance: CloudFront invalidation targets /* to clear all cached files after deploy.

definition_of_done: scripts/deploy-frontend.sh builds and syncs to S3
Cache headers set correctly for hashed assets vs index.html
AWS credentials from GitHub secrets only
CloudFront URL serves latest dashboard after deploy
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md and .claude/constitutions/security.md before starting. The S3 bucket name and CloudFront distribution ID must be read from Terraform outputs — not hardcoded in the script. Use --delete flag on aws s3 sync to remove files that no longer exist in the build output.
